title: The Support of Semiclassical Measures in Higher Dimensions
speaker: Elena Kim
abstract:
Motivated by the quantum unique ergodicity conjecture, we examine semiclassical measures for Laplace eigenfunctions on compact hyperbolic n
-manifolds. We prove their support must contain the cosphere bundle of a compact immersed totally geodesic submanifold. 
The proof adapts a 2-dimensional argument by Dyatlov and Jin to higher dimensions using Cohen's higher-dimensional fractal uncertainty principle. To explain some of the main proof ideas, we will present work on the toy model of quantum cat maps.  
This is joint work with Nick Miller.
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Semiclassical-measure framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up compact real hyperbolic manifolds, Laplace eigenfunctions, semiclassical quantization and limits, and the basic facts that semiclassical measures are geodesic-flow invariant and supported in the cosphere bundle.

proof_stages.label: QUE motivation and prior support results
proof_stages.summary: Quantum ergodicity, QUE, arithmetic QUE, and earlier fractal-uncertainty-principle support theorems are reviewed to isolate the nonarithmetic higher-dimensional support question and the need for a genuinely higher-dimensional FUP.

proof_stages.label: Higher-dimensional support theorem
proof_stages.summary: Using the coframe bundle, the projection to the cosphere bundle, and a one-parameter unipotent/horocyclic flow, the speaker states that every semiclassical measure contains a specified geodesic-unipotent orbit set, and Ratner/Shah theory identifies this set as the cosphere bundle of a minimal compact immersed totally geodesic submanifold.

proof_stages.label: Quantum-cat-map analogue
proof_stages.summary: To explain the proof mechanism in a simpler discrete model, the hyperbolic phase space, geodesic flow, and Laplacian are replaced by a torus, an integral symplectic matrix, and its quantum cat map, leading to an analogous theorem that the support contains an affine line in a stable or unstable eigendirection.

proof_stages.label: Contradiction and microlocal partition
proof_stages.summary: For the cat-map theorem, the proof assumes every stable/unstable affine line meets the complement of the support and constructs a two-piece partition of unity whose symbols have line-holes and with one piece microlocally supported away from the limiting measure.

proof_stages.label: Porosity and Cohen's higher-dimensional FUP
proof_stages.summary: Line and ball porosity are introduced, Cohen's higher-dimensional fractal uncertainty principle is stated, and pictures of propagated holes under the symplectic dynamics explain why products of partition elements should yield porous sets in stable and unstable directions.

proof_stages.label: Propagated-word calculus and porous symbols
proof_stages.summary: Exact Egorov for quantum cat maps is used to define propagated operators and word products, whose microlocal symbols factor into forward and backward products with projections Omega_+ and Omega_- that are line porous.

proof_stages.label: Norm decomposition and FUP contradiction
proof_stages.summary: The identity is decomposed into sums over words with many or few visits to the bad partition element; the many-visit part vanishes by support away from the semiclassical measure, while the few-visit summands are bounded by the FUP estimate for Omega_+ and Omega_-, yielding the contradiction.
